query_text: What are the three qualities of a triple-A supply chain according to the OECD review?

retrieved_chunks.title: OECD Supply Chain Resilience Review: Navigating Risks
retrieved_chunks.text: 102  OECD SUPPLY CHAIN RESILIENCE REVIEW © OECD 2025 market arbitrage, shifting production and exploiting opportunities in local markets. During COVID-19, GVC firms were more resilient due to this capacity to shift production (Giglioli et al., 2021 [19]; Bonadio et al., 2021[20]). Global firms with the best risk management strategies generally benefit from crises because they respond faster than their competition to disruptions (Sheffi, 2015[2]; Sheffi, 2020[4]). 5.3. Triple-A supply chains can deal with uncertainty and change, while preserving the benefits of open trade Top-performing firms tend to have supply chains that possess three different qualities. Described as “triple- A” supply chains (Lee, 2004[21]), they are (1) agile, meaning that they can react quickly to any change in demand or supply; (2) they are adaptable, as they can adjust over time to structural changes and evolving strategies; and (3) they align the interests of all suppliers in the production network,

retrieved_chunks.title: OECD Supply Chain Resilience Review: Navigating Risks
retrieved_chunks.text: 98  OECD SUPPLY CHAIN RESILIENCE REVIEW © OECD 2025 Supply chains are created and managed by firms, which are well positioned to provide insights on effective approaches to build resilience to shocks and disruptions. This chapter analyses the types of risks faced by firms and identifies successful practices. It highlights the role of “triple-A” supply chains – agile, adaptable and aligned – as a reliable source of resilience. 5 Risk management at the firm-level
